discussion_posts.name: alpharomeo99
discussion_posts.content:
Instead of traversing the entire grid and finding the right cells, only traverse over the edges and find unwanted cells. Mark the unwanted cells. Count the remaining cells.
This way you won't need to DFS or BFS the entire grid which would bring down the overall complexity.

discussion_posts.name: iammateus
discussion_posts.content: While some may initially view this question as a mere repetition of the previous one ("1254. Number of Closed Islands"), it would be incorrect to assume that it does not present a challenge to those who have already solved the prior problem. In my personal experience, I found solving this question to be a great exercise that tested my understanding of both problems. It required me to tweak my previous solution and approach the problem with a fresh perspective to achieve the desired outcome.
